polymarket_events_sample.title: MicroStrategy sells any Bitcoin by ___ ?
polymarket_events_sample.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MicroStrategy sells any of its Bitcoin by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from MSTR and on-chain data, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

polymarket_events_sample.title: Kraken IPO by ___ ?
polymarket_events_sample.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kraken (US-based cryptocurrency exchange) completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, as confirmed by official company announcements or credible news sources. The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by Kraken to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If Kraken merges with another entity, is acquired, or ceases to exist before the market resolves, the market will also resolve to "No".

This market will resolve early if Kraken completes an IPO by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

If Kraken is acquired by another company that is already public, this market will immediately resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

polymarket_events_sample.title: Macron out by...?
polymarket_events_sample.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Emmanuel Macron ceases to be President of France for any length of time between January 2 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET (inclusive). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Macron departs from office before the expiry date, the market should resolve immediately.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from the government of France, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: French President Emmanuel Macron's government instability, highlighted by Michel Barnier's ouster via no-confidence vote on December 4, 2024—the shortest tenure on record—fuels trader sentiment on his potential early exit. Macron responded by appointing centrist ally François Bayrou as prime minister on December 13, who is now forming a new cabinet amid a hung National Assembly from June's snap legislative elections. Fiscal pressures mount with the 2025 budget deadline looming, risking another censure motion from left-wing and far-right blocs. Traders price in modest resignation odds via skin-in-the-game consensus, wary of deadlock but noting Macron's inability to dissolve parliament until mid-2025; watch Bayrou's lineup and budget vote this week for market catalysts.

polymarket_events_sample.title: UK election called by...?
polymarket_events_sample.description: This is a market on predicting the date when the UK general election will be officially called.

markets.question: Will the next UK election be called by March 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the date of the next UK general election is declared by March 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For this market to resolve to "Yes" it is only necessary that the election date be declared, not that the election actually occur within the market timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United Kingdom, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.description:

For this market to resolve to "Yes" it is only necessary that the election date be declared, not that the election actually occur within the market timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United Kingdom, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.description:

For this market to resolve to "Yes" it is only necessary that the election date be declared, not that the election actually occur within the market timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United Kingdom, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.description:

For this market to resolve to "Yes" it is only necessary that the election date be declared, not that the election actually occur within the market timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United Kingdom, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Prime Minister Rishi Sunak's surprise announcement on May 22, 2024, calling a snap UK general election for July 4 has driven trader consensus heavily toward him on Polymarket's "UK election called by...?" market, with odds implying over 99% probability of resolution in his favor as the sitting leader exercising constitutional prerogative. Low Conservative polling, a 20+ point Labour lead under Keir Starmer, and economic headwinds like inflation prompted the move amid speculation of internal Tory pressure or a preemptive strike. Traders note minimal upset risk barring extraordinary events like a no-confidence vote. Key upcoming catalysts include the June 4 ITV leaders' debate and campaign gaffes that could shift broader election odds, though call attribution remains locked.

polymarket_events_sample.title: China x India military clash by...?
polymarket_events_sample.description: This is a market on the likelihood of a military clash between China and India occurring by December 31.

markets.question: China x India military clash by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of China (People's Republic of China) and India between January 29, and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between Chinese and Indian military forces. Hand-to-hand combat with the use of melee weapons between military personnel, will qualify. For example the 2020 Galwan Valley clash and the 2022 Yangtse clash would both count.

Non-violent actions, such as warning shots, artillery fire into uninhabited areas, or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution, however minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not.

Note: the China Coast Guard (CCG) is part of the military, however the Indian Coast Guard (ICG) is not.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent India-China agreement on patrolling arrangements along the Line of Actual Control in Ladakh has driven down trader consensus odds for a military clash, reflecting de-escalation after four years of standoffs since the deadly 2020 Galwan Valley skirmish. Both nations continue infrastructure buildup and troop deployments amid unresolved border disputes spanning 3,500 km, but diplomatic channels remain open, with full disengagement from key friction points like Depsang and Demchok. Upcoming BRICS summit in Russia could facilitate Modi-Xi talks, potentially easing tensions further, though nationalist pressures and terrain patrols sustain low-level risks—traders weigh this against historical restraint despite provocations.

polymarket_events_sample.title: NATO/EU troops fighting in Ukraine by...?
polymarket_events_sample.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if active military personnel officially affiliated with any NATO or EU country enter Ukraine for combat-related military purposes directly pertaining to the ongoing conflict with Russia by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For military personnel to qualify toward a "Yes" resolution, they must be 1) officially acknowledged as active military by the NATO or EU entity or member state they are affiliated with; 2) be publicly acknowledged by NATO or an EU-affiliated entity to have entered Ukraine for a combat-related military purpose directly pertaining to the ongoing conflict with Russia.

For military personnel to qualify toward a "Yes" resolution they need be active duty and acknowledged as described above. Participation in a combat role is necessary for this market to resolve to "Yes" (e.g. military personnel providing training or intelligence support would not qualify toward a "Yes" resolution, however drone pilots or infantry directly attacking Russian troops, or soldiers targeting and downing missiles from Ukrainian soil would qualify toward a "Yes" resolution).

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from NATO, the EU, or member states of either entity, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: NATO's longstanding policy against deploying combat troops in Ukraine anchors low trader consensus for direct NATO or EU fighting involvement by year-end, with odds under 10% on Polymarket reflecting aversion to escalation risks. Recent French President Macron's ceasefire peacekeeping remarks and limited EU training missions inside Ukraine sparked brief speculation, but NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte reaffirmed no frontline roles, echoed by US officials amid ongoing arms support without boots on the ground. Zelenskyy's openness to monitors post-truce adds uncertainty, though base rates from prior proxy conflicts favor restraint. US election on November 5 looms large, as a Trump win could accelerate peace talks, diminishing intervention odds further.

polymarket_events_sample.title: Starmer out by...?
polymarket_events_sample.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Keir Starmer ceases to be the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom for any period of time between February 2 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Keir Starmer's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be the government of the UK, however a consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low near-term odds for UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer exiting office prematurely, anchored by Labour's landslide July 2024 victory and fixed-term parliament rules barring snap elections before 2029. Primary pressures stem from post-budget unpopularity—October's tax hikes and welfare cuts, including winter fuel allowance reductions for most pensioners—driving approval ratings below 35% per recent YouGov polls. Criticism lingers over the government's response to summer far-right riots. Starmer maintains control, evidenced by suspending two MPs for rebelling on welfare votes. Watch Labour's conference (Sept 21-25) and November local by-elections for unity tests that could nudge probabilities.

polymarket_events_sample.title: Ukraine recognizes Russian sovereignty over its territory by...?
polymarket_events_sample.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a deal in which Ukraine agrees to formally recognize Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory is reached between the Russian Federation and Ukraine by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The deal must be a publicly announced mutual agreement. 

If such an agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes", regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

An agreement by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory for any amount of time will count.

An agreement by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory as a precondition of a more comprehensive peace process or deal will qualify, even if the agreement is not finalized or part of a formalized peace deal. The September 8, 1995, “Agreed Basic Principles” between Bosnia and Yugoslavia, which recognized the borders and sovereignty of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and was later formalized through the Dayton Peace Agreement, is an example of a qualifying agreement.

An official unilateral pledge by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory will qualify for a “Yes” resolution even if not part of an agreement with Russia.

The April 19, 2013, Brussels Agreement between Serbia and Kosovo, in which Serbia recognized Kosovo’s de facto administration but did not formally grant de jure recognition, would not be considered a qualifying agreement under this market, as this market requires formal recognition of sovereignty rather than acknowledgment of administrative control.

The primary resolution source for this market will be an official announcement by Ukraine and/or the Russian Federation, however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming an agreement has been reached will also qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ukraine's government maintains a firm stance against recognizing Russian sovereignty over Crimea, Donbas, or annexed eastern regions, as enshrined in its constitution and reiterated by President Zelenskyy amid ongoing frontline stalemates. Recent Russian advances in Donetsk have been incremental and high-cost, with no territorial shifts forcing Kyiv toward concessions, while Western aid—bolstered by a new US package—sustains defenses. Absent active peace talks since early 2022 Istanbul efforts collapsed, trader consensus reflects slim probabilities. Key upcoming catalysts include the January 2025 US administration transition, potential NATO summits, and winter military dynamics that could alter negotiation leverage.

polymarket_events_sample.title: Ukraine election called by...?
polymarket_events_sample.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the next Ukrainian presidential election is scheduled in 2025. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is about whether a date for the next Ukrainian election is announced between February 14 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Whether the election is supposed to take place in 2025 or later will have no effect on the resolution to this market.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the Government of Ukraine however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ukraine's ongoing martial law remains the dominant factor suppressing any presidential election, with parliament extending restrictions through February 2025 via a November vote, constitutionally barring polls until 90 days after its end. President Zelenskyy's authority persists under these provisions despite his formal term expiring in May 2024, affirmed by Western allies amid Russia's invasion. Recent opposition calls and incoming US leadership comments on legitimacy add pressure but face security objections from Kyiv. Traders weigh prolonged war versus potential peace negotiations unlocking votes; monitor mid-February parliamentary session for extensions or shifts signaling election timelines.

polymarket_events_sample.title: Will any country leave NATO by...?
polymarket_events_sample.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any member state formally withdraws from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

A country's exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". That country must either withdraw or submit a notice of denunciation to trigger a "Yes" resolution.

The resolution source will be official information from the relevant government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: NATO's unprecedented unity since Russia's 2022 Ukraine invasion remains the dominant factor suppressing trader odds on any member exiting the alliance by the specified date, with no country ever having formally withdrawn in 75 years. Recent July 2024 Washington summit declarations reaffirmed collective defense commitments from all 32 members, countering earlier skepticism from leaders like Hungary's Viktor Orbán or Turkey's Recep Tayyip Erdoğan. While U.S. election rhetoric, particularly from Donald Trump on burden-sharing, fuels speculation, official statements emphasize alliance strength. Traders price near-zero implied probability, reflecting historical base rates and absent primary signals of departure intent; watch November 2024 U.S. vote and 2025 NATO funding talks for potential shifts.

polymarket_events_sample.title: Ukraine election held by...?
polymarket_events_sample.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if national elections for the parliament and/or presidency of Ukraine are held between February 12 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is based on whether national elections are actually held in Ukraine within the specified dates. Merely scheduling an election will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes".

If elections are officially scheduled for a date outside of 2025, this market will immediately resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of Ukraine, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Martial law imposed since Russia's 2022 invasion bars Ukraine from holding presidential or parliamentary elections under its constitution, anchoring trader consensus toward delayed timelines despite President Zelenskyy's term expiring in May 2024. Zelenskyy has repeatedly affirmed no wartime vote is feasible, prioritizing national security, while parliament extended martial law through November 10, 2024, with another extension vote imminent. Stagnant frontlines and aid dependency heighten uncertainty, as U.S. election outcomes could shift Western support and peace prospects. Traders weigh war duration against constitutional hurdles, with implied probabilities low for polls by year-end but rising for 2025 post-martial law lift.

polymarket_events_sample.title: BitBoy convicted?
polymarket_events_sample.description:
BitBoy was recently arrested due to a warrant issued for threatening communications he sent to a Judge. You can read more about that here: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/22059661159362

This market will resolve to "Yes" if BitBoy (Ben Armstrong) is convicted of any charges related to the emails he sent to Judge Kimberly Childs, by March 31, 2026 ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Convictions must be for criminal charges.

If the case is completely dropped for whatever reason, due to a plea agreement without an admission of guilt, a dismissal, or any other situation occurs in which no judgement is rendered or will be rendered by the court, this market will resolve to "No".

Once an individual is convicted it qualifies for an immediate "Yes" resolution, regardless of later appeals, etc. This market will resolve based on the first conviction in a qualifying case.

The primary resolution source will be official court records, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.
